title: Fix return statements in PyMuPDFScraper
body:
Hi, just a quick fix for two bugs in `PyMuPDFScraper`:

1. In case of problems, there were no `return` statements in the except blocks, that caused the function to implicitly return `None`, and that errors out [in here](https://github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher/blob/master/gpt_researcher/scraper/scraper.py#L103C1-L113C22), because `None` can not be unpacked to three variables. I added returning of empty strings/lists, as is done in other scraper classes as well.

2. It was typed to return `str`, but actually it returns `tuple[str, list[str], str]`

step_plan.summary: Update PyMuPDFScraper.scrape to return a consistent 3-tuple on errors and fix its return type annotation from str to tuple[str, list[str], str]. This prevents callers from trying to unpack None and aligns behavior with other scrapers.

steps.operation: Fix return type annotation and add explicit returns in exception handlers
steps.reason: The scrape method is annotated to return str but actually returns a 3-tuple (page_content, image_list, title). Additionally, its except blocks currently only print errors and implicitly return None, which causes unpacking errors in callers that expect a tuple of three elements. Adding an explicit return of the sentinel ("", [], "") in the except branches and correcting the return type annotation to tuple[str, list[str], str] makes behavior consistent with other scrapers and prevents runtime errors when callers unpack the result.
steps.side_effects: Behavioral: callers that previously received None on error will now receive a 3-tuple of empty values ("", [], ""). This is backward-compatible with callers that expect a 3-tuple but may change behavior for code that specifically checks for None to detect failures. Static typing: updating the annotation improves type checking and linter results. Tests: unit tests that asserted None on failure will need updating; tests expecting a 3-tuple are now protected. Resource management: this change does not by itself guarantee temporary files are removed if an exception occurs before os.remove(temp_filename); consider adding cleanup (finally) if persistent temp files are a concern. No public API signature (runtime call pattern) changes other than the corrected annotation and more consistent error return values.
